content.problemStatement:
For a non-empty array CCC containing positive integers, let BBB be a subsequence of array CCC of size MMM (1‚â§M‚â§‚à£C‚à£)(1 \leq M \leq |C|)(1‚â§M‚â§‚à£C‚à£). We are going to play a drawing game based on the sequence BBB:


Select MMM points with **positive integer coordinates** on the XYXYXY ‚àí-‚àí plane such that the points (Bi,0)(B_i, 0)(Bi‚Äã,0), (0,Bi)(0, B_i)(0,Bi‚Äã) and the ithi^{th}ith selected point are collinear. Please note that multiple selected points **can** have the same coordinates.
Next, draw MMM line segments. The ithi^{th}ith line segment should connect the origin (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) with the ithi^{th}ith selected point.
The beauty of a line segment is equal to the number of points on the line segment that have **integer coordinates**. We want to **maximize** the **sum** of beauty over all line segments.

Let W(B)W(B)W(B) denote the **number of ways** to select MMM points based on the sequence BBB such that the sum of beauty over all line segments is **maximized**. Let SSS be the sum of W(B)W(B)W(B) over all non-empty subsequences BBB of the array CCC.

You are given an array AAA of size NNN and QQQ queries. In each query:


LLL RRR : Given two integers LLL and RRR (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N)(1\le L \le R \le N)(1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N), define the array CCC by keeping some elements in the range AL‚Ä¶RA_{L \ldots R}AL‚Ä¶R‚Äã and deleting the rest of the elements from AAA without changing the order. Find out the number of **distinct** values of SSS you can obtain over all the arrays CCC.

Note that the array CCC cannot be empty. In other words, you must keep **at least one** element from the range AL‚Ä¶RA_{L \ldots R}AL‚Ä¶R‚Äã.

Since this number can be quite large, output your answer modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

Input Format

The first line contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN positive integers A1,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã where AiA_iAi‚Äã is the ithi^{th}ith integer of the array AAA.
Each of the next QQQ lines contains two integers LjL_jLj‚Äã and RjR_jRj‚Äã denoting the jthj^{th}jth query.


Output Format
For each query, print the number of **distinct** values of SSS you can obtain, if you can keep some elements in the range AL‚Ä¶RA_{L \ldots R}AL‚Ä¶R‚Äã and then delete the rest from AAA without changing the order modulo (109+7)(10^9+7)(109+7).
constraints: 0‚â§max(A1,‚Ä¶,An)‚àímin(A1,‚Ä¶,An)<1070 \leq max(A_1, \ldots, A_n)-min(A_1, \ldots, A_n) \lt 10^70‚â§max(A1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,An‚Äã)‚àímin(A1‚Äã,‚Ä¶,An‚Äã)<107. In other words, the absolute difference between any two elements of AAA is strictly less than 10710^7107

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Query 111: Given L=1L = 1L=1 and R=3R = 3R=3, the possible arrays CCC made using the subarray A[1,3]A[1, 3]A[1,3] are:

C=[2]C = [2]C=[2]: The only possible subsequence B=[2]B = [2]B=[2]. The only point collinear with (2,0)(2, 0)(2,0) and (0,2)(0, 2)(0,2) that has positive integer coordinates is (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1).
The line segment connecting origin with the selected point (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) can be denoted by the line y=‚àíx+2y = -x + 2y=‚àíx+2.
The number of points lying on this line segment that have integer coordinates are (0,0)(0, 0)(0,0) and (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1). Thus, the beauty is 222.
There is only one way to maximize the beauty, thus the value of W(B)=1W(B) = 1W(B)=1.
Since there is only one possible subsequence, the value of SSS is also 111.
C=[3]:S=2C = [3]: S = 2C=[3]:S=2
C=[4]:S=1C = [4]: S = 1C=[4]:S=1
C=[2,3]:S=5C = [2, 3]: S = 5C=[2,3]:S=5
C=[2,4]:S=3C = [2, 4]: S = 3C=[2,4]:S=3
C=[3,4]:S=5C = [3, 4]: S = 5C=[3,4]:S=5
C=[2,3,4]:S=11C = [2, 3, 4]: S = 11C=[2,3,4]:S=11

There are 555 distinct values of SSS, which are {1,2,3,5,11}\{1, 2, 3, 5, 11\}{1,2,3,5,11}.
